_meta.description: Event isolation (eventId scoping) rules for GEM India. Every database query must filter by eventId to prevent cross-event data leakage.
sources: AGENTS.md — 'Never skip eventId filtering in any database query'
CLAUDE.md — 'Every database query filters by eventId (per-event data isolation)'

core_rule.statement: Every database query MUST include an eventId filter. No query may return or mutate data from a different event than the active one.
core_rule.source: AGENTS.md: 'NEVER skip eventId filtering in any database query. Every query must scope to the active event.'

scoping_mechanism.description: The active eventId is determined by the event workspace context. All dashboard modules operate within the scope of the currently selected event.
scoping_mechanism.source: research-hub/PROJECT_HANDOFF.md — Navigation graph: Event workspace hub (M21)

rules.rule: SELECT queries MUST include WHERE eventId = :activeEventId
INSERT statements MUST set eventId to the active event
UPDATE statements MUST include WHERE eventId = :activeEventId
DELETE statements MUST include WHERE eventId = :activeEventId
API routes MUST extract eventId from the authenticated session/context and pass it to all database operations
Switching events in the workspace MUST reload all data for the new eventId. Stale data from a previous event MUST NOT be visible.
Cascade handlers (Inngest) MUST include eventId in the event payload and use it for all downstream queries

negative_tests.description: Query people list without eventId filter — MUST return error or empty, never data from another event
Create a record with eventId A, switch to event B, verify the record is NOT visible
Attempt to update a record belonging to event A while in event B context — MUST fail

exceptions.description: The events list page (M02) itself shows all events the user has access to, not scoped to a single eventId. This is the entry point BEFORE an event is selected.
exceptions.source: research-hub/PROJECT_HANDOFF.md — Navigation graph: Events List is top-level, before workspace entry
